Create an association by matching the `thumbnailId` value that you provide as a query parameter to this call with the `thumbnailId` field of a [doc://com.apple.classkitcatalogapi/documentation/ClassKitCatalogAPI/Context/Data-data.dictionary] object that you upload with a call to [doc://com.apple.classkitcatalogapi/documentation/ClassKitCatalogAPI/Create-or-Replace-Contexts]. Follow the same guidelines for creating thumbnails as you follow when working with the ClassKit API in your app. In particular, provide images that are at least 80 x 80 pixels, and no larger than 330 x 330 pixels. The system ignores thumbnails that are too small, and scales down thumbnails that are too large. Use a thumbnail with the maximum dimensions to produce the best result across devices.

If you delete every context that references a thumbnail, the system automatically deletes the thumbnail image as well.

# Create or Replace a Thumbnail

**Web Service Endpoint**

Store an image that represents one of your app’s assignable activities.

## Discussion

You can associate a thumbnail image with one or more contexts. Create an association by matching the `thumbnailId` value that you provide as a query parameter to this call with the `thumbnailId` field of a [doc://com.apple.classkitcatalogapi/documentation/ClassKitCatalogAPI/Context/Data-data.dictionary] object that you upload with a call to [doc://com.apple.classkitcatalogapi/documentation/ClassKitCatalogAPI/Create-or-Replace-Contexts]. Upload the context that references a thumbnail before uploading the thumbnail.

Provide the thumbnail image as binary data in the request body. For example, after obtaining a web token, as [doc://com.apple.classkitcatalogapi/documentation/ClassKitCatalogAPI/authenticating-calls-to-the-classkit-catalog-api] describes, you could use the following `curl` command to upload the image in the file `catalog.png`:

```shell
% curl --request POST \
       --url 'https://classkit-catalog.apple.com/v1/thumbnails?thumbnailId=catalog.png&environment=production' \
       --header 'authorization: Bearer <JWT>' \
       --header 'content-type: image/png' \
       --data-binary '@catalog.png'
```

Follow the same guidelines for creating thumbnails as you follow when working with the ClassKit API in your app. In particular, provide images that are at least 80 x 80 pixels, and no larger than 330 x 330 pixels. The system ignores thumbnails that are too small, and scales down thumbnails that are too large. Use a thumbnail with the maximum dimensions to produce the best result across devices.

If you delete every context that references a thumbnail, the system automatically deletes the thumbnail image as well.

## Uploading Thumbnails

- **Get a Thumbnail**: Fetch the image for one of your app’s assignable activities.
- **Delete a Thumbnail**: Remove one of the images for your app’s assignable activities.
